---
title: "Lecture 13: JavaScript Functions & Event Handling"
subtitle: "{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}"
author: "{{< meta instructor.name >}}"
date: "February 16, 2026"
code-annotations: below
---


## {#title-slide data-menu-title="Title Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="custom-title">
[Lecture 13]{.baby-blue-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle">
[JavaScript Functions & Event Handling]{.white-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Week 4 | Unit II: JavaScript Functions & Events<br>
{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}<br>
{{< meta instructor.name >}}, {{< meta instructor.title >}}
</div>

---

## {data-menu-title="Learning Objectives"}

[Learning Objectives]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Write reusable JavaScript functions
- Distinguish parameters and arguments
- Understand function return values
- Handle browser events with listeners
- Use the event object in interactions
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Why Functions"}

[Why Functions Matter]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
::: {.fragment}
Functions help avoid repeated code
:::
::: {.fragment}
Functions make code easier to test and debug
:::
::: {.fragment}
Functions improve readability and maintenance
:::
:::

```javascript
function greet(name) {
  return `Hello, ${name}`;
}
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Function Syntax"}

[Function Declaration vs Expression]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-3|5-7|9"}
function add(a, b) {
  return a + b;
}

const multiply = function(a, b) {
  return a * b;
};

const square = x => x * x;
```

::: {.fragment .body-text-m .topbr}
`add` is declared; `multiply` is stored in a variable; `square` uses arrow syntax.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Parameters"}

[Parameters, Arguments, Return]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2-4|6"}
function calculateTotal(price, taxRate) {
  const tax = price * taxRate;
  const total = price + tax;
  return total;
}

const bill = calculateTotal(1000, 0.18);
```

- Parameters: `price`, `taxRate`
- Arguments: `1000`, `0.18`
- Return value: final result from function

---

## {data-menu-title="Events Intro"}

[What Are Events?]{.slide-title}

<hr>

An event is an action in the browser:

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- user clicks a button
- user types in an input
- page is loaded
- form is submitted
:::

```javascript
button.addEventListener("click", () => {
  console.log("Clicked");
});
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Common Events"}

[Common DOM Events]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```javascript
element.addEventListener("click", handleClick);
element.addEventListener("change", handleChange);
element.addEventListener("input", handleInput);
form.addEventListener("submit", handleSubmit);
window.addEventListener("load", initPage);
```

::: {.fragment .body-text-m .topbr}
Pick event type based on user interaction you want to capture.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Event Object"}

[Using the Event Object]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1-6"}
form.addEventListener("submit", function(event) {
  event.preventDefault();
  console.log(event.type);
  console.log(event.target);
  console.log("Form submission intercepted");
});
```

- `preventDefault()` stops default behavior
- `target` gives the source element

---

## {data-menu-title="Mini Demo"}

[Interactive Counter Demo]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```html
<button id="incBtn">Increase</button>
<p id="count">0</p>
```

```javascript
let value = 0;
document.getElementById("incBtn").addEventListener("click", () => {
  value++;
  document.getElementById("count").textContent = value;
});
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Live Demo"}

[Live Classroom Demo: Functions & Events]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
Use this interactive page during class to demonstrate:

- Function declaration and return values
- Parameters and arguments with real input
- `click`, `input`, and `submit` events
- `event.preventDefault()` in form handling
- Dynamic DOM updates based on function output
:::

::: {.body-text-l .center-text .topbr}
[Open Demo: Functions & Events](../html/demos/lecture-13-demo.html){target="_blank"}
:::

::: {.fragment .body-text-s .center-text .topbr}
Alternative site path: `/html/demos/lecture-13-demo.html`
:::

::: {.fragment .body-text-s .center-text}
[Open app.js Source](../html/demos/lecture-13/app.js){target="_blank"}
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Best Practices"}

[Best Practices]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Keep functions short and focused
- Use descriptive names like `validateEmail()`
- Avoid inline JS in HTML when possible
- Prefer `addEventListener` over old event attributes
- Handle errors and invalid input safely
:::

---

## {#end-slide data-menu-title="End Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="page-center">
<div class="custom-subtitle">
[Questions?]{.white-text}
</div>
<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Next: Lecture 14 - Conditions & Looping
</div>
</div>
